action.reasoning: Observation: Training failed on CE loss with 'expected scalar type Long but found Int' (masks dtype issue). No mIoU or full timing yet. Analysis: Despite np.int64, albumentations transforms (e.g. RandomResizedCrop) can yield int32/uint8 tensors for masks; DataParallel, ResNet50 FCN, label remapping (0->255, 1-150->0-149), JSONL logging, and predict.py contract are otherwise correct and match all specs (meta.json, original-res masks, 4-GPU). Decision: Add .long() cast in dataset __getitem__ to guarantee LongTensor for loss/metric, bump batch_size to 16 + epochs=5 for better baseline while staying under time/iter budget, run train then submit_eval on best.pt for first mIoU + iteration timing before upgrading to better backbone (e.g. MIT-B5) or augs/scheduler.
